display_name: An unexpected IPv6 default gateway or prefix usually means a rogue Router Advertisement, not a routing change
context: Hosts on a segment intermittently acquire an IPv6 default gateway or prefix that the operator did not configure, causing black-holing or traffic interception
observation: Hosts show two default routers (or an unexpected next-hop link-local) in 'show ipv6 routers' / 'ip -6 route', and IPv6 connectivity flaps
naive_interpretation: The routing protocol or first-hop redundancy (HSRPv6/VRRPv3) is misbehaving and advertising an extra gateway
correct_interpretation: Any node on an IPv6 link can emit Router Advertisements, and hosts will believe them. A rogue RA — from a misconfigured host with IPv6 forwarding/Internet-Connection-Sharing enabled, a stray or unauthorized router, or an attacker — makes hosts install a wrong default gateway and SLAAC a wrong prefix. The tell is that the unexpected gateway is a host/unknown link-local rather than your real router or FHRP virtual address, and that the problem correlates with a specific access port/MAC. This is a Layer 2 security issue, not a routing one: identify the RA source link-local, trace it to a port/MAC, and remediate it; then prevent recurrence with RA Guard on access ports so only authorized router ports may advertise. Correlate the host's 'show ipv6 routers' against the known-good gateway before touching routing config.
why: RAs are unauthenticated by default; hosts accept the most recent valid RA, so a rogue sender can override the legitimate gateway
The fix lives at L2 (RA Guard / ND inspection on access ports), so chasing it in the routing control plane wastes time
Correlating which port/MAC the rogue source link-local lives on pinpoints the offending device

confirmation_commands.purpose: List routers heard on the segment; an unexpected source link-local is the rogue
Check whether RA Guard exists to block rogue RAs, and where it is applied
Inspect RA send/receive state to spot an unexpected advertiser
Multiple default routes with different next-hop link-locals confirm a rogue RA

traps: Re-engineering routing/FHRP when the extra gateway is actually a rogue host RA
Not comparing the advertised router link-local against the known-good gateway/FHRP virtual address
Leaving access ports without RA Guard, so the problem recurs after the first device is removed
Assuming RAs are trustworthy because the segment is 'internal'

sources.title: RFC 4861 — Neighbor Discovery for IPv6 (neighbor cache states 7.3.2)
